fields.description:
## Context
The existing kudos system (Epics 3e9b-afee and 71df-5bd8) includes 5 triggers that silently skip on non-Python stacks. T7 uses Python ast.NodeVisitor. T9 uses ruff (Python-only). T12 uses Python-anchored regexes. T13 and T4 use Python ast.dump() subtree hashing. Ruby and JavaScript developers receive zero kudos from these 5 triggers. This epic replaces them with polyglot equivalents so kudos fires across all supported stacks.

## Success Criteria
- T7 uses lizard -ENS instead of Python ast.NodeVisitor; supports Python, JS/TS, Ruby, Java; 30s timeout; --exclude for test dirs; when lizard absent: TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal
- T9 reads commands.lint (from Epic B); when absent: TRIGGER_SKIPPED; when present, fires on zero-violation result
- T12 uses line-start-anchored regexes covering Python, JS/TS, Ruby, Java syntax for commented-out code detection
- T13 and T4 use jscpd (JSON reporter to temp dir, 30s timeout, exit code parsing); when jscpd absent: TRIGGER_SKIPPED
- All five triggers emit machine-readable TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal conforming to the signal format established by Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8); no shared library imported from Kudos Epic 2
- Validation: sprint acceptance test environment has lizard and jscpd installed; test matrix covers all 5 triggers before/after on non-Python files; before: no kudos for Ruby file; after: kudos fires for quality improvement; Kudos Epic 1 must be present in CI or dispatch_kudos_trigger() mocked

## Dependencies
- Epic B (3818-310d): T9 reads commands.lint; without Epic B, T9 always emits TRIGGER_SKIPPED
- Kudos Epic 1 (3e9b-afee): T13 and T7 call the kudos dispatch function delivered by Kudos Epic 1; runtime blocking dependency (blocks relationship in ticket system)
- Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8): runtime consumer dependency — Epic C conforms to TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal format so the existing event emitter can handle it

## Approach
Replace each of the 5 Python-specific trigger implementations with polyglot equivalents: lizard for AST-based nesting analysis (T7), jscpd for duplicate block detection (T13/T4), language-neutral regexes for commented-out code (T12), and commands.lint config key for lint-clean detection (T9). All triggers emit TRIGGER_SKIPPED when required tooling or config is absent.

## Research Findings
- External integration (lizard): -ENS flags emit nesting/size metrics; supports Python, JS/TS, Ruby, Java, C/C++; `command -v lizard` absence detection; 30s subprocess timeout required
- External integration (jscpd): JSON reporter to temp dir (`--reporters json --output <dir>`); exit code 0 = no duplicates, 1 = duplicates found; parse via `cat <dir>/jscpd-report.json` (file-based, not stdout); 30s subprocess timeout required
- Dependency (commands.lint from Epic B 3818-310d): polyglot lint config key; absent → TRIGGER_SKIPPED; present → fires on zero-violation exit
- Signal format (TRIGGER_SKIPPED): conforms to Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) signal format independently — schema duplicated, not imported, to preserve epic independence

## Scenario Analysis
- lizard binary absent in CI (high, deployment): T7 voids awards; TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal emitted per Kudos Epic 2 schema; document or accept skip as designed
- jscpd binary absent in CI (high, deployment): T13/T4 void awards; TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal emitted per Kudos Epic 2 schema; document or accept skip as designed
- commands.lint key absent from dso-config.conf (high, configuration): T9 always emits TRIGGER_SKIPPED; onboarding/architect-foundation must populate this key per detected stack
- lizard/jscpd subprocess exceeds 30s timeout (medium, runtime): kill and treat as TRIGGER_SKIPPED; explicit timeout handling required with SIGKILL after grace period
- TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal format drift from Kudos Epic 2 schema (high, integration): format validated independently per epic; schema change in Epic 2 silently breaks Epic C signals — add schema conformance test in this epic
- T12 regex false-positive on language-specific block comments (medium, correctness): Python `"""`, Ruby `=begin/=end`, JS `/** */` need language-aware anchors beyond simple line-start matching

## Risk Register (from preplanning)
- Testing (03d4-062a, 988f-933e): External binaries need subprocess mocking
- Maintainability (all story): TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal format must conform to Kudos Epic 2 schema independently
- Reliability (a1f9-2e8b): dispatch_kudos_trigger() from Kudos Epic 1 must be present or mocked in acceptance env

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: external integration (lizard, jscpd CLIs), dependency on another epic (Epic B commands.lint key, Kudos Epic 2 signal schema)
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 6
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: not triggered
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: small scale (default) — per-commit polyglot triggers; not volume-sensitive
